seoTitle: Insert a Signature Into Google Docs: 3 Methods
metaDescription: Add a signature to a Google Docs contract using the drawing tool, the integrated eSignature tool, or an uploaded image of your handwritten signature.
intro: Whether you're sealing a freelance contract or finalizing an HR document, Google Docs gives you a few reliable ways to add a signature. This guide covers drawing one by hand, using the integrated eSignature tool for legally binding fields, and uploading a scanned signature image.

steps.heading: Open the Drawing panel to sign by hand
steps.body: From the Insert menu, open the drawing canvas and use the Scribble tool to sketch a freehand signature directly on screen.

steps.heading: Save the drawing into your document
steps.body: Save the drawing to automatically insert your signature as a vector asset right into the text.

steps.heading: Or use the integrated eSignature tool
steps.body: Use the Google eSignature tool to add automated, legally binding signing fields into your document template.

steps.heading: Or upload a scanned signature image
steps.body: Upload a high-resolution photo or scan of your physical signature and adjust its crop and color contrast to match a clean background.

steps.heading: Position the signature over the signature line
steps.body: Use the In front of text or Wrap text formatting presets to place your signature exactly over the underline mark.

faq.question: Is a hand-drawn signature in Google Docs legally valid?
faq.answer: A drawn or uploaded signature can work for many informal agreements, but for legally binding contracts the integrated eSignature tool is the more appropriate option.

faq.question: Can I reuse the same signature in future documents?
faq.answer: Yes, saving your signature element inside a reusable document layout makes it easy to apply to future paperwork.

faq.question: How do I make an uploaded signature image blend in cleanly?
faq.answer: Adjust the crop and color contrast settings so the image matches a clean white background before positioning it over the signature line.

faq.question: What's the difference between the drawing tool and the eSignature tool?
faq.answer: The drawing tool creates a static, hand-sketched signature image, while the eSignature tool sets up an actual signing field for formal, trackable approval.
